title: geum rivale var. intermedium
intro: Geum rivale var. intermedium, also known as the water avens or marsh avens, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family and native to Europe and parts of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory, making it useful in treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and joint pain. The health benefits of Geum rivale var. intermedium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a herbal tea or used in infusions, and it can also be dried or made into a tincture for its medicinal properties to be utilized.
